AURORA Science Product Gallery Mission About Pre-order

Product · AURORA-1

AURORA-1 specifications

Calibrated against NOAA SWPC's Kp-index feed. 12-channel spectrum-true LED stack. USB-C, 8-hour battery. Hand-assembled in Hayward, California.

SKUAURORA-1
Dimensions180 × 180 × 24 mm
Weight340 g
Light field8 megapixel addressable LED array
Channels12 (oxygen-green 557.7nm, oxygen-red 630nm, N₂⁺ violet 427.8nm + 9 mixers)
Color depth14-bit per channel
Refresh240 Hz
Sync sourceNOAA SWPC Kp-index, 1-minute polling
ConnectivityUSB-C, 802.11ax (2.4 / 5 GHz)
Battery8 hours typical, 14 hours dim
Power5 V / 3 A USB-C, 15 W max
MaterialsAnodized aluminum chassis, diffused acrylic face
OriginHayward, California
Price$189 USD · ships Q3

Every AURORA-1 is bench-calibrated against a reference spectrometer before it leaves the workshop. The factory check-set is one of the iss072 frames, rendered in your unit and matched to the published image's Lab values within ΔE < 2.0. If your AURORA drifts past that threshold, the companion app will ask you to send it in for re-calibration. Five-year guarantee.

Quiet by default. AURORA never blinks, never alerts, never asks for your attention. The light just shifts, the way the sky does. If you want a louder signal, you can opt into a soft pulse on Kp-index 7 and above.

Ready? Pre-order or read about how it works.

Imagery courtesy NASA, public domain. Full citations.
